mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates applied to future cash flows, directly reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer price inflation indicate strong aggregate demand outpacing supply, which forces corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a positive month-over-month change in the industrial production index signals rapid capacity utilization and manufacturing rebound, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes during recovery regimes yield negative 5-day returns in over 65 percent of observations, or regime-specific alpha is not significant at 95 percent confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household consumption capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts drop below 1200 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as financial sectors face reduced demand.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of instances when HOUST is below 1200, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, real interest rates fall and expectations for aggressive central bank tightening increase, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20d returns in at least 55 percent of cases, or the joint signal loses predictive power after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion regimes, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 2.5 percent, confirming robust economic momentum that lifts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5 percent during early-cycle regimes yields negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of observations, or the regime-conditional signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.
